title: Wholesome Spanish Rocket Soup
description: A fermented, glassy‑jelly, kid‑friendly Spanish soup featuring rocket, carrot, cucumber, kombucha, and a hint of coconut milk.
instructions: In a saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté minced garlic until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add diced carrots and cucumber to the saucepan and cook for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in vegetable stock and kombucha, and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low and allow the broth to simmer for 10 minutes to meld flavors.
While the broth simmers, rinse rocket leaves thoroughly and set aside.
In a small bowl, dissolve agar-agar powder in 60 ml cold water, stirring until smooth.
Add the agar-agar mixture to the simmering broth, stirring constantly until it thickens slightly, about 2 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Pour the hot broth into a shallow mold or dish, gently fold in the rocket leaves, and allow it to cool to room temperature.
Refrigerate the mixture for at least 120 minutes until it sets into a glassy jelly‑like soup; serve chilled.
